identity.experience: Self-taught, coding seriously for just over one year
identity.shortBio: Wahb Amir is a 16-year-old self-taught full-stack developer and AI developer based in Pakistan. He builds production-ready web applications, backend services, and AI-powered systems end-to-end — from idea to deployed product. In March 2026 he placed 3rd out of 775 international participants at Hack for Humanity 2026 with EcoLens, an AI waste classifier built entirely solo. In April 2026 he won the Technical Award at Hackonomics 2026 out of 1481 participants with EconoQuest, a browser-based economics simulation with a full RAG advisory pipeline and WebSocket streaming — also built solo on zero cloud budget.

projects.name: EconoQuest — AI Economics Simulator
projects.role: Full-Stack Engineer, AI/RAG Architect & Simulation Designer
projects.award: Technical Award — Hackonomics 2026 (1481 participants, solo, international)
projects.description: Browser-based economics simulation with a full RAG advisory pipeline, Socratic AI advisor, and real-time WebSocket streaming — built solo on free infrastructure. Players govern a nation across 7–8 fiscal years adjusting 8 real policy levers affecting 10 outcome metrics, guided by a Socratic AI (Llama 3.3 70B via Groq) that retrieves context from 99 game-situated RAG chunks in Supabase pgvector and never gives direct answers. Three-tier architecture: React frontend → Fastify gateway (cookie proxying, LRU caching, load balancing) → HuggingFace-hosted RAG/inference services. Pure-Python conflict detector runs before every LLM call. 7-archetype classification system at zero inference cost. WebSocket streaming bug fixed by bypassing React batching via index ref. Zero cloud spend.

projects.name: EcoLens — AI Waste Classifier
projects.award: 3rd Place — Hack for Humanity 2026 (775 participants, solo, international)
projects.description: AI-powered waste classification app built solo in one month. Point a camera at any waste item to receive an AI material label, estimated CO₂/water/energy impact, and gamified points toward a global leaderboard. Full ML pipeline: Next.js serverless API → HuggingFace model endpoint → label normalisation → impact calculation → MongoDB write → achievements engine. OTP auth with JWT in HTTP-only cookies, token rotation, and transactional rollback. Six material categories, confidence-gated scan logging, custom achievements engine. Zero paid API budget.

projects.name: Client & Developer Collaboration Platform
projects.description: Unified workspace where clients request quotes, track progress, message developers, and see real-time GitHub-synced updates. Replaced fragmented email/chat workflows. GitHub Actions + webhooks automatically map commits and PRs to project tasks. Role-based access control, JWT auth, real-time in-app and email notifications.

projects.name: Modern Online Store (BoltForm)
projects.description: Performant e-commerce demo with secure Stripe checkout, mobile-first UX, and a read-only admin dashboard.

keyFacts: Wahb Amir is 16 years old and self-taught
He has been coding seriously for just over one year
He placed 3rd at Hack for Humanity 2026 out of 775 international participants (solo build) with EcoLens
He won the Technical Award at Hackonomics 2026 out of 1481 international participants (solo build) with EconoQuest
EcoLens (eco.wahb.space) is his most recognised project — 3rd place internationally at Hack for Humanity 2026
EconoQuest (econoquest.wahb.space) is his most architecturally complex project — RAG pipeline with Supabase pgvector, Groq Llama 3.3 70B, WebSocket streaming, built on zero cloud budget
He manages his own Linux servers and deploys production apps independently
Primary stack: Next.js, TypeScript, Fastify, Supabase, pgvector, Python, PyTorch, Groq
His portfolio is at wahb.space — all project case studies are at wahb.space/projects
